signals.title: NZD/CAD — G8 Bilateral Theme + Energy Shock
signals.text: NZD/CAD spread hits 1.265% as NZD leads G8 performance while CAD lags. Divergence is driven by BoC Governor Macklem's warning of consecutive hikes due to energy shocks, which is currently being outweighed by broader CAD weakness and NZD's hawkish RBNZ bias (hike priced for 27 May). NZD/USD carry at +150bp vs CAD's -150bp deficit against USD further separates the pair. Implication: NZD outperformance persists until RBNZ meeting or oil price stabilization.

signals.title: USD/JPY — Geopolitical + Carry + Intervention
signals.text: USD/JPY at 156.39 holding below the mid-156.00s as hopes for an imminent end to the US-Iran war weigh on the Dollar. Despite the 300bp Fed-BoJ carry supporting USD, JPY COT LF net short -88,543 (30.6% long) remains a crowded trade vulnerable to short-covering if geopolitical de-escalation continues. BoJ intervention risk remains the primary ceiling. Implication: Carry remains the structural bid, but headline-driven JPY covering limits upside ahead of US NFP.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Positioning + Retail Divergence
signals.text: AUD/USD at 0.7245 shows a sharp divergence between institutional and retail sentiment. COT LF net long +48,299 (74.8% long) indicates heavy institutional backing, while retail is 89% short, fading the 1M +5.23% rally. RBA 4.35% vs Fed 3.75% provides a +60bp carry premium for AUD. Implication: Extreme retail short positioning suggests a potential 'pain trade' higher if NFP data underwhelms, targeting 0.7300.

signals.title: EUR/USD — Correlation Break + US NFP
signals.text: EUR/USD at 1.1749 vs EuroStoxx correlation at +0.720 (z=+1.96σ) marks a significant break from the -0.145 norm. EUR is currently trading as a high-beta risk asset rather than a funding currency. With the Fed on hold at 3.75% and ECB at 2.0% (+175bp USD advantage), the pair's 1M +2.08% gain faces a reality check at today's US NFP release. Implication: EUR/USD upside is structurally limited by the carry deficit unless NFP triggers a broad USD liquidation.

signals.title: USD/CHF — Carry vs Safe-Haven Flow
signals.text: USD/CHF at 0.7792 remains supported by a massive +375bp carry advantage (Fed 3.75% vs SNB 0.0%). VIX at 17.4 (normal volatility) suggests safe-haven demand for CHF is marginal, leaving the pair sensitive to US yield shifts. Bull Flattener regime (2s10s +42.6bp) indicates tightening cycle dominance. Implication: USD/CHF remains a carry-positive long while VIX stays below 25, with watch level at 0.7850.
